scores.fix: Add the cross-class higher-budget significance sweep (already named as the camera-ready gate) to lift the ordering-only limit on the headline trainability claim.
scores.justification: Method discipline remains exemplary (reduction with owned 3-10x threshold gap; pre-registered k* test with exact/permutation p-values and power floor; ground-truth-then-falsification framing; honest withdrawal of the metric-robustness sentence). Held at 5/6 because the central section-4 correlation rests on a 50-iteration budget whose class separations (0.045/0.036, verified at HEAD) the paper concedes are ordering-only.

scores.fix: Add class-mean uncertainty (bootstrap CIs) or the cross-class budget sweep so the ordering claim carries quantitative support.
scores.justification: Every headline number independently recomputed from the HEAD artifacts matches (class means, homogeneous inversion, rho=0.109 with rho=0.007 verified at pinned revision 22b1fda6, k* battery, -0.108, full rest_trap ladder). Held at 5/6 because the section-4 headline remains ordering-only: the data does not reject the null of equal class means and the mixed class's rank is conceded metric- and noise-sensitive.
Abstract compressed to ~325 words with one extractable claim per contribution; section-1 enumerates (1)-(5) crisply including the double negative result. The v9 extraction failure is resolved.

scores.fix: Re-run pub-litsearch to resolver-verify openspiel2019/psro2017 and survey the exploitability-evaluation line's empirical follow-ups plus any other parametric-family game constructions.
scores.justification: The new section-6 paragraph engages OpenSpiel and PSRO/NashConv and cedes the measurable-equilibrium-convergence credit to that tradition, scoping the novelty claim precisely. Held at 4/5: the engagement rests on two memory-entered citations flagged unverified in refs.bib, and the tradition's empirical follow-ups are not surveyed. No litsearch sibling exists; scored on the legacy baseline.

scores.fix: Complete (or remove) the HuggingFace baselines hosting promise and regenerate the 300/300 bootstrap sweep as a committed experiments/ artifact before camera-ready.
scores.justification: The v9 drift defect is verifiably fixed: class means re-derived and Figure 2 re-rendered from HEAD match the paper exactly, so the byte-for-byte claim is literally true. Held at 4/5 for two self-named gaps: baselines hosting still in progress, and the 300/300 sweep + t-interval trace to a PR-review recomputation rather than a regenerable artifact.

scores.fix: Swap the Figure 1 caption clause to 'rows are beta and columns are kappa' (and consider normalizing Figure 1/Figure 2 to one orientation).
scores.justification: v9 defects fixed and verified (CF-2 descriptor correct at all three sites; Table 2 overflow gone; captions otherwise self-contained), but the caption half-sentence added in v10 is transposed: it says rows are kappa and columns are beta while the rendered Figure 1 and its generating script have rows=beta, columns=kappa - a new caption/figure contradiction in the headline figure.

Independent pdflatex+bibtex build: 27 pages, 0 overfull hboxes (v9: 10, five severe), 0 broken cross-references or unresolved citations; structure and tense intact; the xurl/path treatment, display-math reward tuple, and itemized verdict ladder resolve every flagged site. Residual log noise is cosmetic.

All 11 cite keys resolve with complete fields plus arXiv IDs; no orphans; no undefined citations in the final pass; the two new entries are honestly flagged for litsearch/audit verification per the existing precedent; repo-internal results cite committed artifact paths.

scores.fix: Cut the conclusion's restatement of the abstract arc to the two sentences a reader needs after the body; answer the page-budget question at submission time.
scores.justification: The largest v9 duplication is gone (abstract ~700 -> ~325 words; statistics moved to owning sections; caveat restatements collapsed). Held at 3/4: the conclusion still restates the full result arc at near-abstract length, and 13 body pages serve a 4-page workshop brief.

findings.rationale: Figure 1's caption says 'within each panel rows are $\kappa$ and columns are $\beta$' but the rendered figure and figures/src/phase_diagram.py have rows=beta, columns=kappa; the caption contradicts the figure's own axis labels and its own later 'kappa in {0.3,0.7} columns' clause.
findings.suggested_fix: Swap the clause to 'rows are $\beta$ and columns are $\kappa$'.

findings.rationale: The new exploitability-tradition engagement rests on two memory-entered, disk-unverified citations (openspiel2019, psro2017) and does not survey that line's empirical follow-ups. [related-work]
findings.suggested_fix: Re-run pub-litsearch to resolver-verify the new entries and check for additional close prior work; also source the Appendix A named-template citations.

findings.rationale: The unnumbered conclusion restates the abstract's full result arc at comparable coverage.
findings.suggested_fix: Tighten to what-was-established + open-challenge; deduplicate against the abstract.

findings.rationale: The 300/300 bootstrap-combination sweep and t-interval trace to the PR #460 judge recomputation, not a regenerable experiments/ artifact (honestly footnoted).
findings.suggested_fix: Regenerate as a committed script + JSON before camera-ready.

findings.rationale: HuggingFace baselines hosting remains 'in progress'; the NeurIPS checklist expects it resolved by submission.
findings.suggested_fix: Complete or remove the hosting promise before submission (operator task).

findings.rationale: Header comment says the abstract was compressed 'to ~260 words'; the rendered abstract is ~325 words.
findings.suggested_fix: Sync the header comment (cosmetic; never renders).

findings.rationale: xurl breaks caption-resident artifact paths without hyphens ('ex periments'), which can be misparsed as a space in the path.
findings.suggested_fix: Optionally shorten/alias caption paths; acceptable cost of the 0-overfull fix.

findings.rationale: Tagged display equations (S)/(A)/(C) repeat in Appendix B, producing duplicate hyperref destination warnings.
findings.suggested_fix: Silence with hypertexnames=false or distinct tags if the audit log-cleanliness bar tightens.
